docstring.text: Public model exports for PyRIT core data structures and helpers.

Enum representing the possible outcomes of an attack.

Inherits from ``str`` so that values serialize naturally in Pydantic
models and REST responses without a dedicated mapping function.
Base class for all attack results.

Return the main conversation ID plus pruned (user-visible) related conversation IDs.

Excludes adversarial chat conversations which are internal implementation details.

returns.desc: set[str]: Main + pruned conversation IDs.

docstring.text: Return the main conversation ID plus all related conversation IDs.

returns.desc: set[str]: All conversation IDs associated with this attack.

docstring.text:
Return the attack strategy identifier from the composite atomic identifier.

This is the non-deprecated replacement for the ``attack_identifier`` property.
Extracts and returns the ``"attack"`` child from ``atomic_attack_identifier``.

returns.desc:
Optional[ComponentIdentifier]: The attack strategy identifier, or ``None`` if
``atomic_attack_identifier`` is not set.

returns.desc: True if the conversation is part of this attack.

attributes.name: attack_identifier
attributes.docstring:
Deprecated: use ``get_attack_strategy_identifier()`` or ``atomic_attack_identifier`` instead.

Returns the attack strategy ``ComponentIdentifier`` extracted from
``atomic_attack_identifier``, emitting a deprecation warning.

Returns:
    Optional[ComponentIdentifier]: The attack strategy identifier, or ``None``.

params.name: path
params.desc: Blob URL or path to test.

docstring.text:
Asynchronously reads the content of a file (blob) from Azure Blob Storage.

If the provided `path` is a full URL
(e.g., "https://account.blob.core.windows.net/container/dir1/dir2/sample.png"),
it extracts the relative blob path (e.g., "dir1/dir2/sample.png") to correctly access the blob.
If a relative path is provided, it will use it as-is.

params.name: path
params.desc:
The path to the file (blob) in Azure Blob Storage.
                    This can be either a full URL or a relative path.

returns.desc: The content of the file (blob) as bytes.

raises.desc:
If there is an error in reading the blob file, an exception will be logged
    and re-raised.

docstring.text: Write data to Azure Blob Storage at the specified path.

params.name: path
params.desc: The full Azure Blob Storage URL

params.name: data
params.desc: The data to write.

params.desc: Azure Blob container URL.

params.name: sas_token
params.desc: Optional SAS token.

params.name: blob_content_type
params.desc: Blob content type for uploads.

raises.desc: If container_url is missing.

docstring.text:
Initialize a generic binary-path serializer.

This serializer handles generic binary data that doesn't fit into specific
categories like images, audio, or video. Useful for XPIA attacks and
storing files like PDFs, documents, or other binary formats.

params.desc: The category or context for the data.

params.name: prompt_text
params.desc: The binary file path or identifier.

params.name: extension
params.desc: The file extension, defaults to 'bin'.

docstring.text:
A harm definition loaded from a YAML file.

This class represents the structured content of a harm definition YAML file,
which includes the version, category name, and scale descriptions that define
how to score content for this harm category.

params.name: version
params.desc: The version of the harm definition (e.g., "1.0").

params.name: category
params.desc: The harm category name (e.g., "violence", "hate_speech").

params.name: scale_descriptions
params.desc: List of scale descriptions defining score levels.

params.name: source_path
params.desc: The path to the YAML file this was loaded from.

docstring.text:
Load and validate a harm definition from a YAML file.

The function first checks if the path is a simple filename (e.g., "violence.yaml")
and if so, looks for it in the standard HARM_DEFINITION_PATH directory.
Otherwise, it treats the path as a full or relative path.

params.name: harm_definition_path
params.desc:
Path to the harm definition YAML file.
Can be a simple filename like "violence.yaml" which will be resolved
relative to the standard harm_definition directory, or a full path.

returns.desc: The loaded harm definition.

raises.desc: If the harm definition file does not exist.
If the YAML file is invalid or missing required fields.

docstring.text: Get the description for a specific score value.

params.name: score_value
params.desc: The score value to look up (e.g., "1", "2").

returns.desc: The description for the score value, or None if not found.

docstring.text:
Validate a harm category name.

Validates that the category name follows the naming convention (lowercase letters
and underscores only) and optionally checks if it exists in the standard
harm definitions.

params.name: category
params.desc: The category name to validate.

params.name: check_exists
params.desc:
If True, also verify the category exists in
get_all_harm_definitions(). Defaults to False.

returns.desc: True if the category is valid (and exists if check_exists is True),
False otherwise.

docstring.text:
Represents a message in a conversation, for example a prompt or a response to a prompt.

This is a single request to a target. It can contain multiple message pieces.

Create a deep copy of this message with new IDs and timestamp for all message pieces.

This is useful when you need to reuse a message template but want fresh IDs
to avoid database conflicts (e.g., during retry attempts).

The original_prompt_id is intentionally kept the same to track the origin.
Generates a new timestamp to reflect when the duplicate is created.

returns.desc: A new Message with deep-copied message pieces, new IDs, and fresh timestamp.

docstring.text: Flatten messages into a single list of message pieces.

params.name: messages
params.desc: Messages to flatten.

returns.desc: MutableSequence[MessagePiece]: Flattened message pieces.

params.name: prompt
params.desc: Prompt text.

params.name: role
params.desc: Role assigned to the message piece.

params.name: prompt_metadata
params.desc: Optional prompt metadata.

returns.desc: Constructed message instance.

docstring.text: Build a message from a system prompt.

params.name: system_prompt

returns.desc: Constructed system-role message.

returns.desc: A list of matching MessagePiece objects (may be empty).

Set that the prompt is not in the database.

This is needed when we're scoring prompts or other things that have not been sent by PyRIT

Set the role of all message pieces to simulated_assistant.

This marks the message as coming from a simulated conversation
rather than an actual target response.
Convert the message to a dictionary representation.

A dictionary with 'role', 'converted_value', 'conversation_id', 'sequence',
and 'converted_value_data_type' keys.

docstring.text: Validate that all message pieces are internally consistent.

raises.desc:
If piece collection is empty or contains mismatched conversation IDs,
sequence numbers, roles, or missing converted values.

attributes.name: api_role
attributes.docstring:
Return the API-compatible role of the first message piece.

Maps simulated_assistant to assistant for API compatibility.
All message pieces in a Message should have the same role.

Returns:
    ChatMessageRole: Role compatible with external API calls.

Raises:
    ValueError: If the message has no pieces.

attributes.name: conversation_id
attributes.docstring:
Return the conversation ID of the first request piece.

Returns:
    str: Conversation identifier.

Raises:
    ValueError: If the message has no pieces.

attributes.name: is_simulated
attributes.docstring:
Check if this is a simulated assistant response.

Simulated responses come from prepended conversations or generated
simulated conversations, not from actual target responses.

attributes.name: role
attributes.docstring:
Deprecated: Use api_role for comparisons or _role for internal storage.

This property is deprecated and will be removed in a future version.
Returns api_role for backward compatibility.

attributes.name: sequence
attributes.docstring:
Return the sequence value of the first request piece.

Returns:
    int: Sequence number for the message turn.

Raises:
    ValueError: If the message has no pieces.

params.name: message_pieces
params.desc: Pieces belonging to the same message turn.

params.name: skip_validation
params.desc: Whether to skip consistency validation.

raises.desc: If no message pieces are provided.

docstring.text:
Represents a piece of a message to a target.

This class represents a single piece of a message that will be sent
to a target. Since some targets can handle multiple pieces (e.g., text and images),
requests are composed of lists of MessagePiece objects.

Get the actual stored role, including simulated_assistant.

Use this when duplicating messages or preserving role information
for storage. For API calls or comparisons, use api_role instead.

returns.desc: The actual role stored (may be simulated_assistant).

docstring.text: Check if the message piece has an error.

returns.desc: True when the response_error is not "none".

docstring.text: Check if the message piece is blocked.

returns.desc: True when the response_error is "blocked".

docstring.text:
Set that the prompt is not in the database.

This is needed when we're scoring prompts or other things that have not been sent by PyRIT

Compute SHA256 hash values for original and converted payloads.
It should be called after object creation if `original_value` and `converted_value` are set.

Note, this method is async due to the blob retrieval. And because of that, we opted
to take it out of main and setter functions. The disadvantage is that it must be explicitly called.
Convert this message piece to a dictionary representation.

returns.desc: dict[str, object]: Dictionary representation suitable for serialization.

docstring.text: Convert this message piece into a Message.

returns.desc: A Message containing this piece.

attributes.name: api_role
attributes.docstring:
Role to use for API calls.

Maps simulated_assistant to assistant for API compatibility.
Use this property when sending messages to external APIs.

attributes.name: is_simulated
attributes.docstring:
Check if this is a simulated assistant response.

Simulated responses come from prepended conversations or generated
simulated conversations, not from actual target responses.

attributes.name: role
attributes.docstring:
Deprecated: Use api_role for comparisons or _role for internal storage.

This property is deprecated and will be removed in a future version.
Returns api_role for backward compatibility.

docstring.text: Initialize a MessagePiece.

params.name: role
params.desc: The role of the prompt (system, assistant, user).

params.name: original_value
params.desc: The text of the original prompt. If prompt is an image, it's a link.

params.name: original_value_sha256
params.desc: The SHA256 hash of the original prompt data. Defaults to None.

params.name: converted_value
params.desc: The text of the converted prompt. If prompt is an image, it's a link. Defaults to None.

params.name: converted_value_sha256
params.desc: The SHA256 hash of the converted prompt data. Defaults to None.

params.name: id
params.desc: The unique identifier for the memory entry. Defaults to None (auto-generated).

params.name: conversation_id
params.desc:
The identifier for the conversation which is associated with a single target.
Defaults to None.

params.name: sequence
params.desc: The order of the conversation within a conversation_id. Defaults to -1.

params.name: labels
params.desc: The labels associated with the memory entry. Several can be standardized. Defaults to None.

params.name: prompt_metadata
params.desc:
The metadata associated with the prompt. This can be specific to any scenarios.
Because memory is how components talk with each other, this can be component specific.
e.g. the URI from a file uploaded to a blob store, or a document type you want to upload.
Defaults to None.

params.name: converter_identifiers
params.desc:
The converter identifiers for the prompt. Can be ComponentIdentifier
objects or dicts (deprecated, will be removed in 0.14.0). Defaults to None.

params.name: prompt_target_identifier
params.desc: The target identifier for the prompt. Defaults to None.

params.name: attack_identifier
params.desc: The attack identifier for the prompt. Defaults to None.

params.name: scorer_identifier
params.desc:
The scorer identifier for the prompt. Can be a ComponentIdentifier or a
dict (deprecated, will be removed in 0.13.0). Defaults to None.

params.name: original_value_data_type
params.desc: The data type of the original prompt (text, image). Defaults to "text".

params.name: converted_value_data_type
params.desc: The data type of the converted prompt (text, image). Defaults to "text".

params.name: response_error
params.desc: The response error type. Defaults to "none".

params.name: originator
params.desc: The originator of the prompt. Defaults to "undefined".

params.name: original_prompt_id
params.desc: The original prompt id. It is equal to id unless it is a duplicate. Defaults to None.

params.name: timestamp
params.desc: The timestamp of the memory entry. Defaults to None (auto-generated).

params.name: scores
params.desc: The scores associated with the prompt. Defaults to None.

params.name: targeted_harm_categories
params.desc: The harm categories associated with the prompt. Defaults to None.

raises.desc: If role, data types, or response error are invalid.

docstring.text:
Normalize a scenario name to match the stored class name format.

Converts CLI-style snake_case names (e.g., "foundry" or "content_harms") to
PascalCase class names (e.g., "Foundry" or "ContentHarms") for database queries.
If the input is already in PascalCase or doesn't match the snake_case pattern,
it is returned unchanged.

This is the inverse of ScenarioRegistry._class_name_to_scenario_name().

params.name: scenario_name
params.desc: The scenario name to normalize.

returns.desc: The normalized scenario name suitable for database queries.

docstring.text: Get the success rate of this scenario.

params.name: atomic_attack_name
params.desc:
Name of specific atomic attack to calculate rate for.
If None, calculates rate across all atomic attacks. Defaults to None.

returns.desc: Success rate as a percentage (0-100).

docstring.text: Represents a normalized score generated by a scorer component.

Return the value of the score based on its type.

If the score type is "true_false", it returns True if the score value is "true" (case-insensitive),
otherwise it returns False.

If the score type is "float_scale", it returns the score value as a float.

returns.desc: bool | float: Parsed score value.

raises.desc: If the score type is unknown.

docstring.text: Convert this score to a dictionary.

returns.desc: Dict[str, Any]: Serialized score payload.

docstring.text:
Render self.value as a template with provided parameters. For parameters in the template
that are not provided as kwargs here, this function will leave them as is instead of raising an error.

params.name: kwargs
params.desc: Key-value pairs to replace in the SeedPrompt value.

returns.desc: A new prompt with the parameters applied.

raises.desc: If parameters are missing or invalid in the template.

docstring.text:
Compute the SHA256 hash value asynchronously.
It should be called after prompt `value` is serialized to text,
as file paths used in the `value` may have changed from local to memory storage paths.

Note, this method is async due to the blob retrieval. And because of that, we opted
to take it out of main and setter functions. The disadvantage is that it must be explicitly called.

attributes.name: data_type
attributes.docstring:
Return the data type for this seed.

Base implementation returns 'text'. SeedPrompt overrides this
to support multiple data types (image_path, audio_path, etc.).

docstring.text:
A group of seeds for use in attack scenarios.

This class extends SeedGroup with attack-specific validation:
- Requires exactly one SeedObjective (not optional like in SeedGroup)

All other functionality (simulated conversation, prepended conversation,
next_message, etc.) is inherited from SeedGroup.

Validate the seed attack group state.

Extends SeedGroup validation to require exactly one objective.

raises.desc: If validation fails.

attributes.name: objective
attributes.docstring:
Get the objective for this attack group.

Unlike SeedGroup.objective which may return None, SeedAttackGroup
guarantees exactly one objective exists.

Returns:
    The SeedObjective for this attack group.

docstring.text: Initialize a SeedAttackGroup.

params.name: seeds
params.desc: Sequence of seeds. Must include exactly one SeedObjective.

raises.desc: If seeds is empty.
If exactly one objective is not provided.

docstring.text:
A group of seeds representing a general attack technique.

This class extends SeedGroup with technique-specific validation:
- Requires all seeds to have is_general_technique=True

All other functionality (simulated conversation, prepended conversation,
next_message, etc.) is inherited from SeedGroup.

Validate the seed attack technique group state.

Extends SeedGroup validation to require all seeds to be general strategies.

raises.desc: If validation fails.

docstring.text: Initialize a SeedAttackTechniqueGroup.

params.name: seeds
params.desc: Sequence of seeds. All seeds must have is_general_technique=True.

raises.desc: If seeds is empty.
If any seed does not have is_general_technique=True.

docstring.text:
SeedDataset manages seed prompts plus optional top-level defaults.
Prompts are stored as a Sequence[Seed], so references to prompt properties
are straightforward (e.g. ds.seeds[0].value).
Build a SeedDataset by merging top-level defaults into each item in `seeds`.

params.name: data
params.desc: Dataset payload with top-level defaults and seed entries.

returns.desc: Constructed dataset with merged defaults.

raises.desc: If any seed entry includes a pre-set prompt_group_id.

docstring.text: Extract and return random prompt values from the dataset.

params.name: number
params.desc: The number of random prompt values to return.

params.name: harm_categories
params.desc:
If provided, only prompts containing at least one of
these harm categories are included.

returns.desc: Sequence[str]: A list of prompt values.

docstring.text: Extract and return prompt values from the dataset.

params.name: first
params.desc: If provided, values from the first N prompts are included.

params.name: last
params.desc: If provided, values from the last N prompts are included.

params.name: harm_categories
params.desc:
If provided, only prompts containing at least one of
these harm categories are included.

returns.desc: Sequence[str]: A list of prompt values.

docstring.text:
Group the given list of seeds by prompt_group_id and create
SeedGroup or SeedAttackGroup instances.

For each group, this method first attempts to create a SeedAttackGroup
(which has attack-specific properties like objective). If validation fails,
it falls back to a basic SeedGroup.

params.name: seeds
params.desc: A list of Seed objects.

returns.desc: A list of SeedGroup or SeedAttackGroup objects, with seeds grouped by
prompt_group_id. Each group will be ordered by the sequence number of
the seeds, if available.

docstring.text: Render seed values as templates using provided parameters.

params.name: kwargs
params.desc: Key-value pairs to replace in the SeedDataset value.

raises.desc: If parameters are missing or invalid in the template.

attributes.name: objectives
attributes.docstring:
Return all objective-type seeds.

Returns:
    Sequence[SeedObjective]: Objective seeds in this dataset.

attributes.name: prompts
attributes.docstring:
Return all prompt-type seeds.

Returns:
    Sequence[SeedPrompt]: Prompt seeds in this dataset.

attributes.name: seed_groups
attributes.docstring:
Returns the seeds grouped by their prompt_group_id.

Returns:
    Sequence[SeedGroup]: A list of SeedGroup objects, with seeds grouped by prompt_group_id.

docstring.text:
Initialize the dataset.
Typically, you'll call from_dict or from_yaml_file so that top-level defaults
are merged into each seed. If you're passing seeds directly, they can be
either a list of Seed objects or seed dictionaries (which then get
converted to Seed objects).

params.name: seeds
params.desc: List of seed dictionaries or Seed objects.

params.name: data_type
params.desc: Default data type for seeds.

params.name: name
params.desc: Name of the dataset.

params.name: dataset_name
params.desc: Dataset name for categorization.

params.name: harm_categories
params.desc: List of harm categories.

params.name: description
params.desc: Description of the dataset.

params.name: authors
params.desc: List of authors.

params.name: groups
params.desc: List of groups.

params.name: source
params.desc: Source of the dataset.

params.name: date_added
params.desc: Date when the dataset was added.

params.name: added_by
params.desc: User who added the dataset.

params.name: seed_type
params.desc: The type of seeds in this dataset ("prompt", "objective", or "simulated_conversation").

params.name: is_objective
params.desc: Deprecated in 0.13.0. Use seed_type="objective" instead.

raises.desc: If seeds are missing or contain invalid/contradictory seed definitions.

docstring.text:
A container for grouping prompts that need to be sent together.

This class handles:
- Grouping of SeedPrompt, SeedObjective, and SeedSimulatedConversation
- Consistent group IDs and roles across seeds
- Prepended conversation and next message extraction
- Validation of sequence overlaps between SeedPrompts and SeedSimulatedConversation

All prompts in the group share the same `prompt_group_id`.
Check if this is a single text prompt.

returns.desc: True when there is exactly one prompt and it is text.

docstring.text: Check if all prompts are in a single sequence.

returns.desc: True when all prompts share one sequence number.

docstring.text: Check if this is a single-turn group (single request without objective).

returns.desc: True when the group is a single request and has no objective.

docstring.text: Render seed values as templates with provided parameters.

params.name: kwargs
params.desc: Key-value pairs to replace in seed values.

docstring.text:
Validate the seed group state.

This method can be called after external modifications to seeds
to ensure the group remains in a valid state. It is automatically
called during initialization.

raises.desc: If validation fails.

attributes.name: harm_categories
attributes.docstring:
Returns a deduplicated list of all harm categories from all seeds.

Returns:
    List of harm categories with duplicates removed.

attributes.name: has_simulated_conversation
attributes.docstring: Check if this group uses simulated conversation generation.

attributes.name: next_message
attributes.docstring:
Returns a Message containing only the last turn's prompts if it's a user message.

Returns:
    Message for the current/last turn if user role, or None otherwise.

attributes.name: objective
attributes.docstring: Get the objective for this group.

attributes.name: prepended_conversation
attributes.docstring:
Returns Messages that should be prepended as conversation history.

Returns all messages except the last user sequence.

Returns:
    Messages for conversation history, or None if empty.

attributes.name: prompts
attributes.docstring: Get all SeedPrompt instances from this group.

attributes.name: simulated_conversation_config
attributes.docstring: Get the simulated conversation configuration if set.

attributes.name: user_messages
attributes.docstring:
Returns all prompts as user Messages, one per sequence.

Returns:
    All user messages in sequence order, or empty list if no prompts.

docstring.text: Initialize a SeedGroup.

params.name: seeds
params.desc:
Sequence of seeds. Can include:
- SeedObjective (or dict with seed_type="objective")
- SeedSimulatedConversation (or dict with seed_type="simulated_conversation")
- SeedPrompt for prompts (or dict with seed_type="prompt" or no seed_type)
Note: is_objective and is_simulated_conversation are deprecated since 0.13.0.

raises.desc: If seeds is empty.
If multiple objectives are provided.
If SeedPrompt sequences overlap with SeedSimulatedConversation range.

docstring.text: Represents a seed objective with various attributes and metadata.

Load a Seed from a YAML file. Because SeedObjectives do not have any parameters, the required_parameters
and error_message arguments are unused.

params.name: template_path
params.desc: Path to the YAML file containing the template.

params.name: required_parameters
params.desc: List of parameter names that must exist in the template.

params.name: error_message
params.desc: Custom error message if validation fails. If None, a default message is used.

returns.desc: The loaded and validated seed of the specific subclass type.

docstring.text: Represents a seed prompt with various attributes and metadata.

Convert a list of Messages to a list of SeedPrompts.

Each MessagePiece becomes a SeedPrompt. All pieces from the same message
share the same sequence number, preserving the grouping.

params.name: messages
params.desc: List of Messages to convert.

params.name: starting_sequence
params.desc: The starting sequence number. Defaults to 0.

params.name: prompt_group_id
params.desc: Optional group ID to assign to all prompts. Defaults to None.

returns.desc: List of SeedPrompts with incrementing sequence numbers per message.

docstring.text: Load a Seed from a YAML file and validate that it contains specific parameters.

params.name: template_path
params.desc: Path to the YAML file containing the template.

params.name: required_parameters
params.desc: List of parameter names that must exist in the template.

params.name: error_message
params.desc: Custom error message if validation fails. If None, a default message is used.

returns.desc: The loaded and validated SeedPrompt of the specific subclass type.

raises.desc: If the template doesn't contain all required parameters.

docstring.text:
Set encoding metadata for the prompt within metadata dictionary. For images, this is just the
file format. For audio and video, this also includes bitrate (kBits/s as int), samplerate (samples/second
as int), bitdepth (as int), filesize (bytes as int), and duration (seconds as int) if the file type is
supported by TinyTag. Example supported file types include: MP3, MP4, M4A, and WAV.

Configuration for generating a simulated conversation dynamically.

This class holds the paths and parameters needed to generate prepended conversation
content by running an adversarial chat against a simulated (compliant) target.

This is a pure configuration class. The actual generation is performed by
`generate_simulated_conversation_async` in the executor layer, which accepts
this config along with runtime dependencies (adversarial_chat target, scorer).

The `value` property returns a JSON serialization of the config for database
storage and deduplication.
Compute a deterministic hash of this configuration.

returns.desc: A SHA256 hash string representing the configuration.

docstring.text: Create a SeedSimulatedConversation from a dictionary, typically from YAML.

params.name: data
params.desc: Dictionary containing the configuration.

returns.desc: A new SeedSimulatedConversation instance.

raises.desc: If required configuration fields are missing.

docstring.text: Load a SeedSimulatedConversation from a YAML file and validate required parameters.

params.name: template_path
params.desc: Path to the YAML file containing the config.

params.name: required_parameters
params.desc: List of parameter names that must exist.

params.name: error_message
params.desc: Custom error message if validation fails.

returns.desc: The loaded and validated SeedSimulatedConversation.

raises.desc: If required parameters are missing.

docstring.text: Get an identifier dict capturing this configuration for comparison/storage.

returns.desc: Dictionary with configuration details.

docstring.text:
Load and render the simulated target system prompt.

If no path is provided, returns None (no system prompt).
Validates that the template has required `objective` and `num_turns` parameters.

params.name: objective
params.desc: The objective to render into the template.

params.name: num_turns
params.desc: The number of turns to render into the template.

params.name: simulated_target_system_prompt_path
params.desc:
Optional path to the prompt YAML file.
If None, no system prompt is used.

returns.desc: The rendered system prompt string, or None if no path is provided.

raises.desc: If the template doesn't have required parameters.

attributes.name: sequence_range
attributes.docstring:
Get the range of sequence numbers this simulated conversation will occupy.

Each turn generates 2 messages (user + assistant), so num_turns generates
num_turns * 2 messages. If next_message_system_prompt_path is set, an additional
user message is added at the end.

Returns:
    A range object representing the sequence numbers.

docstring.text: Initialize a SeedSimulatedConversation.

params.name: adversarial_chat_system_prompt_path
params.desc:
Path to YAML file containing the adversarial
chat system prompt.

params.name: simulated_target_system_prompt_path
params.desc:
Optional path to YAML file containing
the simulated target system prompt. Defaults to the compliant prompt.

params.name: next_message_system_prompt_path
params.desc:
Optional path to YAML file containing the system
prompt for generating a final user message. If provided, after the simulated
conversation is generated, a single LLM call generates a user message that
attempts to get the target to fulfill the objective. Defaults to None
(no next message generation).

params.name: num_turns
params.desc: Number of conversation turns to generate. Defaults to 3.

params.name: sequence
params.desc:
The starting sequence number for generated turns. When combined with
static SeedPrompts, this determines where the simulated turns are inserted.
Defaults to 0.

params.name: pyrit_version
params.desc: PyRIT version for reproducibility tracking. Defaults to current version.

params.name: **kwargs
params.desc: Additional arguments passed to the Seed base class.

raises.desc: If num_turns is not positive or sequence is negative.

docstring.text: Enum for predefined simulated target system prompt paths.
Abstract interface for storage systems (local disk, Azure Storage Account, etc.).
Asynchronously creates a directory or equivalent in the storage system if it doesn't exist.
Asynchronously checks if the path refers to a file (not a directory or container).
Asynchronously checks if a file or blob exists at the given path.
Asynchronously reads the file (or blob) from the given path.
Asynchronously writes data to the given path.
Base class for all strategy results.
Create a deep copy of the result.

returns.desc: A deep copy of the result.

Indicate whether this serializer persists data on disk.

params.desc: Prompt value.

params.name: data_type
params.desc: Text-like prompt data type.

docstring.text:
Score is an object that validates all the fields. However, we need a common
data class that can be used to store the raw score value before it is normalized and validated.
Convert this unvalidated score into a validated Score.

params.name: score_value
params.desc: Normalized score value.

params.name: score_type
params.desc: Score type.

returns.desc: Validated score object.

Indicate whether this serializer persists data on disk.

returns.desc: Always True for video path serializers.

params.name: category
params.desc: The category or context for the data.

params.name: prompt_text
params.desc: The video path or identifier.

params.name: extension
params.desc: The file extension, defaults to 'mp4'.

params.name: request
params.desc: Source request message piece.

params.name: response_text_pieces
params.desc: Response values to include.

params.name: response_type
params.desc: Data type for original and converted response values.

params.name: prompt_metadata
params.desc: Additional metadata to merge.

params.name: error
params.desc: Error classification for the response.

docstring.text: Create a DataTypeSerializer instance.

params.name: data_type
params.desc: The type of the data (e.g., 'text', 'image_path', 'audio_path').

params.name: value
params.desc: The data value to be serialized.

params.name: extension
params.desc: The file extension, if applicable.

params.desc: The category or context for the data (e.g., 'seed-prompt-entries').

returns.desc: An instance of the appropriate serializer.

raises.desc: If the category is not provided or invalid.

docstring.text:
Load all harm definitions from the standard harm_definition directory.

This function scans the HARM_DEFINITION_PATH directory for all YAML files
and loads each one as a HarmDefinition.

returns.desc:
Dict[str, HarmDefinition]: A dictionary mapping category names to their
HarmDefinition objects. The keys are the category names from the YAML files
(e.g., "violence", "hate_speech").

raises.desc: If any YAML file in the directory is invalid.

docstring.text:
Example:
>>> message_pieces = [
>>>     MessagePiece(conversation_id=1, sequence=1, text="Given this list of creatures, which is your
>>>     favorite:"),
>>>     MessagePiece(conversation_id=1, sequence=2, text="Good question!"),
>>>     MessagePiece(conversation_id=1, sequence=1, text="Raccoon, Narwhal, or Sloth?"),
>>>     MessagePiece(conversation_id=1, sequence=2, text="I'd have to say raccoons are my favorite!"),
>>> ]
>>> grouped_responses = group_conversation_message_pieces(message_pieces)
... [
...     Message(message_pieces=[
...         MessagePiece(conversation_id=1, sequence=1, text="Given this list of creatures, which is your
...         favorite:"),
...         MessagePiece(conversation_id=1, sequence=1, text="Raccoon, Narwhal, or Sloth?")
...     ]),
...     Message(message_pieces=[
...         MessagePiece(conversation_id=1, sequence=2, text="Good question!"),
...         MessagePiece(conversation_id=1, sequence=2, text="I'd have to say raccoons are my favorite!")
...     ])
... ]

params.name: message_pieces
params.desc:
A list of MessagePiece objects representing individual
message pieces.

returns.desc:
MutableSequence[Message]: A list of Message objects representing grouped message
pieces. This is ordered by the sequence number.

raises.desc:
If the conversation ID of any message piece does not match the conversation ID of the first
message piece.

docstring.text:
Example:
>>> message_pieces = [
>>>     MessagePiece(conversation_id="conv1", sequence=1, text="Hello"),
>>>     MessagePiece(conversation_id="conv2", sequence=1, text="Hi there"),
>>>     MessagePiece(conversation_id="conv1", sequence=2, text="How are you?"),
>>>     MessagePiece(conversation_id="conv2", sequence=2, text="I'm good"),
>>> ]
>>> conversations = group_message_pieces_into_conversations(message_pieces)
>>> # Returns a list of 2 conversations:
>>> # [
>>> #   [Message(seq=1), Message(seq=2)],  # conv1
>>> #   [Message(seq=1), Message(seq=2)]   # conv2
>>> # ]

params.name: message_pieces
params.desc:
A list of MessagePiece objects from
potentially different conversations.

returns.desc:
list[list[Message]]: A list of conversations, where each conversation is a list
of Message objects grouped by sequence.

docstring.text:
Group by conversation_id.
Order conversations by the earliest timestamp within each conversation_id.
Within each conversation, order messages by sequence.

params.name: message_pieces
params.desc: Message pieces to sort.

returns.desc: list[MessagePiece]: Sorted message pieces.
